title: Topological Viability Principle
canonical_statement: Recoverable transformational futures remain viable only if they remain sufficiently connected, traversable, and rewovable under recursive constraint.
core_question: How is viable accessibility connected, traversable, fragmented, and rewoven?

conditions.connectedness: Recoverable transformational accessibility remains structurally connected.
conditions.traversability: Admissible survivable pathways exist between recoverable regions.
conditions.rewovability: Fragmented accessibility fibers can restore recoverable continuity through repair dynamics.

failure_mode: Topological viability fails when recoverable accessibility connectivity degenerates beyond traversable or rewovable continuity.
one_sentence_lock: Accessibility Topology studies whether recoverable transformational futures remain connected, traversable, and rewovable under recursive constraint.
